description: Defines a column in a tracetable by combining a shape reference, property name, and display label. Also supports function definitions with a variable name instead of a shape property.
utility: Define a tracetable column that tracks a shape property or function value

arguments.name: reference
arguments.description: The shape or function to track — e.g., a point, circle, angle, vector, line, or user-defined function

arguments.name: property / variable
arguments.description: For shapes: property name to extract (e.g., 'x', 'radius', 'degrees', 'sin'). For functions: variable name to evaluate with (e.g., 'x', 't')

arguments.name: label
arguments.description: Display label for the column header. Supports AsciiMath notation for math rendering.

alternates.description: Track x coordinate of point A
Track y coordinate of point A
Track f(x) as variable x changes. f must be a function definition.

examples.scenario: Track trig ratios of an angle
examples.expression: tracetable(at(10, 6), slidingwindow(10), field(angle_1, 'degrees', "θ°"), field(angle_1, 'sin', "sin θ"), field(angle_1, 'cos', "cos θ"), field(angle_1, 'tan', "tan θ"))

examples.scenario: Track a function and shape together
examples.expression:
f(x) = x^2
tracetable(at(10, 6), slidingwindow(10), field(A, 'x', "x"), field(f, 'x', "f(x)"), field(C, 'radius', "R"))

notes: Used exclusively inside tracetable() — not a standalone expression
For shapes: the property name must be a valid property for that shape type (same properties as p() function)
For functions: the variable name must exist in the expression context and have a current numeric value

Supported shape properties include: point (x, y), line (distance, slope), circle (radius, diameter, area, circumference), vector (magnitude, direction), angle (degrees, sin, cos, tan, csc, sec, cot), triangle (area, in_radius, circum_radius), polygon (perimeter, num_sides, angle), conic (eccentricity, semimajor, semiminor, etc.)
